unit_09 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_09

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 492 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 SE
While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i) are also (ii) : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance. [(hfSARS: ke INGRE)While catalogs of record labels that specialize in Native American music are useful indices of Native American consumers' preferences, their (i) are also (ii) : some genres of Native American music have been deemed inappropriate to record because of their special cultural significance. [(hfSARS: ke INGRE)
答案：B, D　omissions; telling
deemgenrerecord
deem: standard unit headword | genre: standard unit headword | record: standard unit headword
#2 SE
In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.In a field of egotists, Bloomfield is ______, always praising her competitors and punctuating her correspondence with self-deprecating remarks.
答案：A, F　unassuming; modest
punctuateunassumingmodest
punctuate: standard unit headword | unassuming: standard unit headword | modest: standard unit headword

答案：B　a neutrality
predilectionpublication
predilection: standard unit headword | publication: standard unit headword
#4 TC TRIPLE
A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.A transformative scientific idea that emerged in the eighteenth century was the realization that slow, inexorable geological processes follow the basic laws of physics and chemistry. This seems (i)______ conclusion in hindsight, but its implication—that geological processes in the distant past must have (ii)______ these very same laws—was (iii)______ geologists in the eighteenth and nineteenth centuries.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　an obvious; followed; revolutionary for
inexorablehindsight
inexorable: standard unit headword | hindsight: standard unit headword

Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)______ the credibility of scientific work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　elicits skepticism; invalidate; vitiate

答案：D　boast of

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　presided over; undisputed; insufficient

答案：E　antithesis

#13 TC TRIPLE

Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Indeed, one of the noteworthy features of our time is that the relentless (i) __________ of the power of science is paralleled by a growing sense of (ii) __________ about science. Anyone old enough to recall the public's enthusiasm for so many of the scientific breakthroughs in the 1950s and 1960s will be struck by the more (iii) __________ even fearful, attitude toward scientific developments at present.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　expansion; distrust; grudging
indeedparallel
indeed: standard unit headword | parallel: standard unit headword
#14 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
insidiousemploy
insidious: standard unit headword | employ: standard unit headword

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　stranger in; alienation; birthplace

答案：E　youthfulness
ample

The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. Less (i)______ is the cowbirds’ habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)______ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
comical

Blank 3

Blank 3

#49 SE

答案：C, E　minimal; trifling
trifling
trifling: standard unit headword
#50 TC TRIPLE
Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.Perhaps the best one can say of this new book of criticism is that it is scintillatingly (i) __________. The author is adept at dealing with trivial details in the poets he studies, but he is quite (ii) __________ when it comes to illuminating the overall vision. The verbal intricacy and panache of his writing contrast with its conceptual (iii) __________. If there are very few authentic ideas in the poems under consideration, there is also a shortage of them in the author's commentary.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　shallow; indifferent; poverty

panache: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：B, F　supplies; strong bargaining positions

答案：E　haphazard

With Easterners still (i) __________ the representation of the West, western writers had to publish in the East in order to succeed, despite a (ii) __________ literary scene in many western cities. It is part of (ii) in the museum’s architecture and design department, which in the past has championed architecture’s artistic value over its real-world consequences.

If you wish to make a novel (i)______ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)______ received opinion, which can (iii)______ a nascent idea before it can develop.If you wish to make a novel (i)______ a field of study, you must master what scholars have already said on the subject but at the same time keep in mind the (ii)______ received opinion, which can (iii)______ a nascent idea before it can develop.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　contribution to; oppressive influence of; quash

While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.Shark teeth are among the most common of all vertebrate fossils. While the abundance of teeth may (i) __________ creatures renowned for their voracious appetites, it contrasts sharply with the (ii) __________ of shark skeletons in the fossil record.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　befit; rarity

答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable

#158 TC TRIPLE
When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.When a new scientific model emerges, research studies (i)______ that paradigm tend to dominate in the scientific literature: the process of selecting articles for publication is tilted toward positive results. But once the paradigm (ii)______, the academic incentives shift in the opposite direction: research results are more likely to be considered worthy of publication when they (iii)______ what has become the established view.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　affirming; has become entrenched; undermine

#159 SE

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

Blank 1
Blank 2

#161 TC SINGLE

答案：B　unwarranted

Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. If the data undermined positions she had previously (ii) __________, she followed the data. If the data confirmed her earlier views, she clearly stated the fact. If the data were (iii) __________, she counseled caution.Although often criticized by former allies for having abandoned or betrayed their common cause, Nguyen was actually remarkably (i) __________ over the years. Examination of her career shows that she never ceased to use the best data she could find to illuminate the issues. Blank 3
答案：A, D, H　consistent; espoused; insufficient

答案：B　decoupled from

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　esoteric; parochial

egalitarian: standard unit headword

答案：C　aspirational

Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.Many researchers assume that politicians consider the preferences of the entire public, and not merely those of likely voters, when making decisions. Yet, since the desire to be reelected is central, it is plausible that politicians (i)______ the opinions of likely voters in formulating their positions on issues. It is possible that researchers’ suppositions about policy makers’ (ii)______ aggregate public opinion may serve to (iii)______ the fact that only the preferences of likely voters actually matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　attend disproportionately to; responsiveness to; mask
assume
assume: standard unit headword

答案：A　unctuous

The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.Faced with a drastic loss of revenue, executives of local newspapers belong to two strategic camps: the (i) __________ and the (ii) __________. The former, trying to do more of the same but more cheaply, are doomed. Only the latter, embracing a fundamentally different way of doing business, will prosper in the face of unstoppable change.

Blank 2
答案：A, D　economizers; visionaries

While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility

答案：A　ancient lineages

One of the great misconceptions regarding Earth is that it is (i) . This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. This is true only if your sense of connection depends on large chunks of physical matter moving from place to place. The dust and rocks that rain down from space are indeed (ii) , even if some of the larger rocks are occasionally a (iii) . Matter trickles in, trickles out. But matter is not everything: an unending spate of pure luminous energy pours down on Earth in a 170,000-trillion-watt torrent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　isolated; negligible; cause for concern
indeed
indeed: standard unit headword
#305 SE
Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.Experiments show that it is shockingly easy to elicit a sense of ______ among a group of strangers: just tell them they’ll be working as a team, and they immediately start working as a team.
答案：D, E　solidarity; camaraderie

It provides second chances, in other words. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace

record: standard unit headword

答案：C, D　untethered from; divorced from

答案：B, D, I　inverted; defend her findings; concluded

But it probably spent much of its time (ii) : its big teeth were suited for catching fish, while its flipper-like feet must have been (iii) walking. Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. This is not (iii)______ view, and we must be grateful to Schechter for putting it forward.Schechter is atypically (i)______ the film version of Stephen King's horror novel The Shining because the qualities for which the majority of other critics have approved it (its artful camera work and so on) get in the way of narrative and render the story less, rather than more, (ii)______ than other films of the same genre. 答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace

At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i) effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii) those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii) in its effects on habitat.At least one otter species, the sea otter, has a large, often dominating, effect on the structure of its own habitat. There is no evidence that other species have ever exercised such (i) effects. That lack of evidence could merely be because the other species have been studied less thoroughly. However, the size and density of the historic sea otter populations in many Pacific coastal regions (ii) those of other otter species elsewhere, so perhaps the sea otter is indeed (iii) in its effects on habitat.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　significant; vastly exceed; unique

What rings false is his picture of the impresarios as unerring Machiavellian schemers-villains who never (iii) __________ in their efforts to pile up a fortune and ruin the world of music. However, my experience in the concert and recording world suggests that there is more bumbling than sinister plotting.Lebrecht recounts many entertaining scandals, (i) __________ himself on some points in deference to the laws of libel. His anecdotes seem (ii) __________ but when carefully scrutinized, their accuracy is less than impressive. 答案：C, F　insubstantial; superficial

答案：D, F　soft; malleable
